verdict_summary: Six confirmed defects across input handling, routing, visibility, and fallback. Zero false positives. CLI is reliable only for marker-matching single-line prompts under the OCS LLM cognition branch.

findings.title: Multi-line prompts are fragmented into separate turns
findings.root_cause: Single Python input() call per turn; no bracketed-paste handling, no continuation/EOF sentinel, no --prompt-file. Blank lines silently discarded.
findings.operational_impact: Each newline of a pasted block becomes an independent turn; routing, OCS cognition, and fallback are applied per fragment.

findings.title: Routing is a manually ordered cascade of fragile string predicates
findings.root_cause: No unified intent classifier; each predicate uses its own substring search with no ambiguity scoring or priority tie-breaker.
findings.operational_impact: Phrasing-sensitive routing produces inconsistent destinations without operator-visible explanation.

findings.title: providers: NONE is a turn-summary projection gap
findings.branch: turn-summary construction at end of every routing branch
findings.root_cause: Only _interactive_ocs_llm_cognition_turn_summary populates provider_ids; all other branches omit it, so _interactive_turn_providers returns [] and the renderer prints NONE.
findings.operational_impact: Completion artifact asserts no providers were involved even when a provider was attempted and failed.

findings.title: elapsed_seconds is a hardcoded stage count
findings.root_cause: _interactive_turn_elapsed_seconds returns artifact['stage_count'] (constant 8). _duration_history returns last_duration_seconds=1 per stage. No time.monotonic measurement.
findings.operational_impact: elapsed: 8s is invariant; replay-visible elapsed_seconds carries no real latency information.

findings.title: TURN COMPLETED is recorded before result delivery
findings.branch: completion block at end of try-body
findings.root_cause: record_conversational_result_delivered is called inside _record_interactive_turn_completion, which stamps delivered_at=created_at and result_delivered=True BEFORE terminal_output_writer flushes the buffer.
findings.operational_impact: result_delivered: TRUE is asserted prior to the operator-facing print; process death between artifact write and flush leaves replay inconsistent with terminal output.

findings.title: Progress checkpoints are deterministic placeholders
findings.root_cause: All checkpoints use snapshot_at=created_at (CLI constant). Five cognition checkpoints are emitted unconditionally even for branches that don't run those stages. elapsed_seconds derived from snapshot - started is always 0.
findings.operational_impact: Progress visibility reflects the lifecycle template, not real execution; operators cannot diagnose stalls from the rendered output.

findings.title: Provider-unavailable clarification fallback is a hard-coded phrase list
findings.root_cause: Fallback recognizes only 'workstation'+create/open/build/add and the literal phrases 'improve trading'/'add analysis'/'create reporting' (with optional period). Everything else fails closed.
findings.operational_impact: Whenever the provider is unavailable, almost every prompt produces 'prompt is not clarification-eligible' instead of a graceful clarification or offline response.

findings.title: Default conversation path requires a live OpenAI provider
findings.root_cause: Hard default to OPENAI_PROVIDER_ID + OpenAIProviderAdapter() with no offline/deterministic fallback transport. The OCS branch has its own in-process transport but the legacy conversation branch does not.
findings.operational_impact: CLI is unusable offline; non-marker prompts fall into a branch that requires AIGOL_OPENAI_API_KEY.

findings.title: Operator decision-support keyword soup can collide with OCS markers
findings.branch: elif chain in aigol/cli/aigol_cli.py at 1735 and 2216
findings.root_cause: Both predicates can match the same prompt; routing is decided by source-file ordering rather than ambiguity scoring or explicit priority.
findings.operational_impact: Latent ambiguity; nearly identical prompts produce different artifact types without operator-visible diagnostics.

findings.title: Per-turn created_at is a CLI argument, not a real timestamp
findings.branch: created_at extraction at top of run_interactive_conversation
findings.root_cause: created_at is taken from args.created_at (default 2026-06-01T00:00:00Z) and reused for every artifact across every turn. No real-clock derivation.
findings.operational_impact: Forensic correlation between operator wall-clock observations and replay artifacts is impossible.

operator_questions_resolved.why_multi_line_split_into_turns: F-1: single input() per turn; no multi-line aggregation.
operator_questions_resolved.stdin_parsing_buffering_or_turn_allocati: Stdin parsing — line-buffered input(). Turn allocation is itself correct, but it is invoked once per stdin line.
operator_questions_resolved.why_some_turns_run_OCS_others_clarificat: F-2 + F-7 + F-8: OCS branch fires only on narrow markers; everything else falls to the OpenAI-dependent default branch whose failure fallback only recognizes four phrases.
operator_questions_resolved.routing_deterministic: Yes, but extremely phrasing-sensitive (F-2). Same prompt always routes the same way; small phrasing changes can flip routing.
operator_questions_resolved.when_providers_NONE_vs_alpha_beta: F-3: only _interactive_ocs_llm_cognition_turn_summary populates provider_ids; every other branch produces NONE regardless of whether providers were attempted.
operator_questions_resolved.is_elapsed_time_measured_correctly: No (F-4 + F-6 + F-10). It is a constant equal to stage_count (8) and is unrelated to real wall-clock duration.
operator_questions_resolved.is_TURN_COMPLETED_emitted_only_after_res: No (F-5). The result_delivered artifact is recorded before terminal_output_writer flushes.
operator_questions_resolved.does_progress_visibility_reflect_real_ex: No (F-6 + F-10). Checkpoints carry constant timestamps and are emitted regardless of whether the corresponding stage actually ran.
operator_questions_resolved.is_CLI_operationally_reliable_for_normal: No. Reliable only for marker-matching single-line prompts under the OCS-cognition branch with an available provider transport.
